Smooth green sponge

Kingdom Animalia

Updated Oct 2016

Where seen?

This smooth sponge forms a layer over coral rubble and is often seen on our Southern shores. **

Features

A thin layer encrusting coral rubble 10-20cm. Generally a smooth layer but also often with irregular knobs, folds and lobes. Tiny circular holes at the top of folds and elsewhere. Texture glossy, rubbery with tiny bumps, visible both underwater and out of water. Usually light green, also greyish blue. Often lighter at the edges of the protruding portions.

May be confused with Blue icing sponge ( Lendenfeldia cf. chondrodes ) which may also be green, but is very smooth and doesn’t have tiny bumps on the surface. Also similar to the Smooth brown sponges ( Neopetrosia exigua ) which are brown.

Pulau Salu, Aug 10
Terumbu Semakau, Dec 11
Labrador, Apr 10

**Species are difficult to positively identify without close examination.

On this website, they are grouped by external features for convenience of display.*

Smooth green sponges on Singapore shores

On wildsingapore flickr

  • Photos for free download
  • Map of sightings in Singapore

Other sightings on Singapore shores

References

  • Lim Swee Cheng, Nicole de Voogd and Tan Koh Siang. 2008. A Guide to Sponges of Singapore . Singapore Science Centre. 173pp.
